title: cronsplain schedule golden vectors: candidates/vectors.js (candidate-agnostic interface + ground truth)
description: Candidate-agnostic golden-vector file for the schedule tournament (mirrors mdtoc's vectors.py role). Authored BEFORE any candidate. Fixes the nextOccurrences interface + the hand-computed ground truth so candidates (T-047/T-048) and the verdict (T-049) can self-check. Depends on T-045: it require()s lib/parser.js to build parsed inputs. Imports NO candidate.
acceptance_criteria: OWNS EXACTLY: projects/cronsplain/candidates/vectors.js. Touch no other file. require()s ../lib/parser (parser.parse) to build parsed inputs. Imports NO schedule_*.js candidate.

INTERFACE (documented in the module docstring; every candidate implements it): nextOccurrences(parsed, fromDate, count) -> Date[]. Returns the next `count` UTC Date objects whose minute matches `parsed` (the parse-result from parser.parse), each strictly AFTER fromDate (EXCLUSIVE), minute-aligned (seconds=0, ms=0), ascending. Applies DOM/DOW OR coupling: when parsed.domRestricted && parsed.dowRestricted, a day matches if day-of-month OR day-of-week matches; if only one restricted, only that field constrains; if neither, all days.

VECTORS: array of ground-truth cases { expr, from (ISO-Z string), count, expected (array of ISO-Z strings) }. MUST cover: (a) simple step '*/15 * * * *'; (b) daily '0 0 * * *'; (c) month rollover (a late-in-month match rolling to next month); (d) YEAR rollover (Dec 31 -> Jan 1); (e) leap Feb 29 '0 0 29 2 *' -> only leap years; (f) DOM/DOW OR quirk '0 0 13 * 5' (13th OR Friday) and '0 0 1 * 1' (1st OR Monday); (g) one-field-* cases (dom restricted with dow=*, and dow restricted with dom=*); (h) EXCLUSIVITY: a `from` landing exactly on a matching minute must NOT return that minute (returns the next). Each expected value hand-computed and annotated with a // comment citing the rule it exercises (OR-coupling / exclusivity / rollover / leap).

runAgainst(nextOccurrencesFn) -> { passed:int, total:int, failures:[{expr, expected, got}] }: for each VECTOR, parse expr via parser.parse, call nextOccurrencesFn(parsed, new Date(from), count), map returned Dates to ISO-Z (d.toISOString(), which is already Z; ensure seconds=0), compare to expected; accumulate. Imports NO candidate (the fn is passed in). module.exports = { VECTORS, runAgainst, INTERFACE_DOC }.
Provide at least ~10 VECTORS total spanning the categories above. The `expected` arrays are the TRUSTED GROUND TRUTH — compute them carefully by hand and cite each.
SHARED CONVENTIONS: see T-044 acceptance item. CommonJS, zero deps, UTC everywhere.
HARNESS PROTOCOL: claim/lock(candidates/vectors.js)/in_progress/handoff --to-role verifier; NEVER self-mark done; --agent <you>.

VERIFY (in handoff note): from projects/cronsplain/ `node -e "const v=require('./candidates/vectors'); console.log(typeof v.runAgainst, Array.isArray(v.VECTORS), v.VECTORS.length)"` prints "function true <N>" with N>=10.

notes.note: plan: author candidates/vectors.js (candidate-agnostic, requires only lib/parser). Define nextOccurrences(parsed, fromDate, count) interface doc, ~11 hand-computed golden VECTORS spanning simple step, daily+exclusivity, month rollover, year rollover, leap Feb29, DOM/DOW OR quirk (13th-or-Friday, 1st-or-Monday), one-field-star cases both directions, end-of-month short-month skip, step-across-range. runAgainst(fn) harness + self-check main. Will cross-check every expected array against a throwaway scratch reference implementation before shipping (not committed).

EXPECTED: candidates/vectors.js requires only ../lib/parser (no candidate import), exports VECTORS/runAgainst/INTERFACE_DOC, self-check standalone run prints count and exits 0, and every hand-computed expected[] is independently reproducible against real cron/Gregorian semantics. ACTUAL: matches. node candidates/vectors.js -> self-check OK, VECTORS=11, expected occurrences total=38, exit 0. node -e (const v=require) verify command -> printed 'function true 11' (N=11 >= 10, satisfies acceptance item). File touches ONLY projects/cronsplain/candidates/vectors.js (git status confirms no other cronsplain source file changed by this task).

HANDOFF -> verifier: HANDOFF -> verifier. OWNS EXACTLY projects/cronsplain/candidates/vectors.js (git status confirms no other file in the repo touched by this task). INTERFACE PINNED (module docstring + INTERFACE_DOC export): nextOccurrences(parsed, fromDate, count) -> Date[] ; returns exactly count UTC Dates (Date.UTC-built, seconds=0 ms=0), each STRICTLY AFTER fromDate (exclusive boundary), ascending, matching parsed.fields.{minute,hour,month} via plain Set membership and applying DOM/DOW coupling: both restricted -> union (dayOfMonth OR dayOfWeek); exactly one restricted -> only that field constrains; neither restricted -> every day matches. VECTOR INVENTORY (11 cases, 38 total expected occurrences, all categories from the T-046 acceptance criteria covered): (a) simple step */15 * * * * [1]; (b) daily 0 0 * * * + exclusivity-at-from [1]; (c) month rollover 0 0 28 * * (day-28 safe from short-month edge, from lands on match to also reinforce exclusivity) [1]; (d) year rollover 0 0 1 1 * Dec31->Jan1 [1]; (e) leap Feb29 0 0 29 2 * (2023->2024 leap->2028 leap, skips non-leap 2025/2026/2027) [1]; (f) DOM/DOW OR quirk: 0 0 13 * 5 (13th-or-Friday, July2026: Fri 3/10/17/24/31 plus 13th which IS a Monday proving union pulls in a dom-only day) [1] and 0 0 1 * 1 (1st-or-Monday, from lands exactly on July1 which matches via DOM only so exclusivity+OR both exercised together; Mon 6/13/20/27 plus Aug1 which is a Saturday proving union again) [1]; (g) one-field-* both directions: 0 0 15 * * dom-restricted/dow=* [1] and 0 9 * * 1-5 dow-restricted/dom=* weekend-skip [1]; bonus end-of-month skip 0 0 31 * * (Feb/Apr have no 31st, skipped entirely, from lands on match) [1]; bonus step-across-range 0 9-17/4 * * * hours {9,13,17} with daily rollover after last slot [1]. Every weekday used in comments (2026-07-01=Wed .. 2026-08-01=Sat) is derived from the closed-form dow=(3+dayOfYear) mod 7 anchored on 2026-01-01=Thursday (itself derived from 2024-01-01=Monday via +366 then +365 day mod-7 shifts), cited inline in the module docstring. SELF-CHECK DISCLOSURE (per task instructions, NOT shipped): wrote a throwaway independent brute-force reference nextOccurrences (plain minute-tick scan using only Date.UTC/getUTC* getters and the same domRestricted/dowRestricted OR-rule, written WITHOUT reusing any of the hand day-of-week arithmetic used to author the vectors) at <scratchpad>/15636cc2-22dd-4ee8-8047-516cb2b65a41/scratchpad/scratch_reference.js, ran it via runAgainst(nextOccurrences) against every VECTOR: result = 11/11 passed, 0 failures (observed output: 'scratch-reference cross-check: 11/11 vectors passed' / 'ALL VECTORS CONFIRMED against independent brute-force reference', exit 0). REPLAY COMMANDS (from projects/cronsplain/): (1) node candidates/vectors.js -> observed: 'cronsplain schedule golden vectors (T-046) -- self-check OK', VECTORS=11, expected occurrences total=38, exit 0. (2) node -e "const v=require('./candidates/vectors'); console.log(typeof v.runAgainst, Array.isArray(v.VECTORS), v.VECTORS.length)" -> observed: 'function true 11' (matches the acceptance item's required pattern, N=11>=10). (3) to replay the scratch cross-check yourself, the reference script is disposable/not part of the deliverable; a verifier can write an equivalent brute-force nextOccurrences and call require('./candidates/vectors').runAgainst(fn) directly, or trust the disclosed 11/11 result above. CRITERIA CHECKLIST: OWNS-EXACTLY(candidates/vectors.js only, no candidate import) OK; INTERFACE documented in docstring+INTERFACE_DOC export OK; VECTORS array shape {expr,from,count,expected} OK; all 8 required categories (a-h) present OK; runAgainst(fn)->{passed,total,failures:[{expr,expected,got}]} imports no candidate OK; >=10 vectors with hand-computed+cited expected values OK (11 provided); CommonJS/zero-deps/UTC OK; standalone runnable node candidates/vectors.js prints count and exits 0 OK; importable via require OK. DEVIATIONS: none from the T-046.json acceptance criteria; added 2 bonus vectors (end-of-month short-month skip, step-across-range) beyond the minimum for extra tournament robustness — flagged here as ADDITIVE, not a substitution for any required category. Locks released after this handoff.

VERIFIED (independent replay). node candidates/vectors.js -> self-check OK, VECTORS=11, expected total=38, exit 0. Import: node -e require -> 'function true 11 string'. DATA AUDIT: wrote my OWN from-scratch minute-tick brute-force (scratchpad/indep_check.js) using the verified parser only for field Sets + my own DOM/DOW OR-coupling; recomputed all 11 expected arrays -> ALL 11 MATCH exactly (no reuse of producer scratch). Weekdays (2026-07-01=Wed..2026-08-01=Sat) and leap classification (2024/2028 leap; 2025/26/27 common) independently confirmed via JS Date getUTCDay. OR-quirk vectors confirmed UNION not intersection: '0 0 13 * 5' pulls in 07-13 (a Monday, dom-only) alongside Fridays 3/10/17/24/31; '0 0 1 * 1' pulls in Aug-1 (Saturday, dom-only) alongside Mondays. Leap Feb29 -> 2024 then 2028 (skips 2025/26/27). End-of-month '0 0 31 * *' skips Feb/Apr -> Mar/May/Jul. Exclusivity: 4 vectors have from == a matching instant (0 0 *, 0 0 28, 0 0 1 *1, 0 0 31) and correctly return the NEXT match. Step '9-17/4' -> {9,13,17}. runAgainst NOT a rubber stamp: wrongStub(from+i min)->0/11 (11 failures); exclusivity-break stub->7/11 failing exactly the 4 from-on-match vectors; myCorrectFn->11/11. DISCIPLINE: git shows only untracked projects/cronsplain/candidates/vectors.js as source (others are .harness bookkeeping); require()s only ../lib/parser, imports no candidate; no lock on vectors.js (producer released, handoff clean); backtick command examples in notes are benign docs. HOUSEKEEPING: lock.py status shows T-050 (substrate-worker-3) explain.js+test_explain.js live; per task instruction did NOT sweep (T-050 worker live); nothing expired.
